canonical_question: What is the AWS security checklist?
aliases: AWS IAM S3 EC2 security checklist
how to secure my AWS account

temporal_validity.last_breaking_change: CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark v3.0.0 (2024); AWS SSE-S3 default encryption for new buckets (Jan 2023)

constraints: NEVER use AWS root account for daily operations -- enable MFA and lock it away
NEVER embed long-lived access keys in code, environment variables, or config files -- use IAM roles with temporary credentials
NEVER allow wildcard (*) Actions or Resources in IAM policies for production workloads
NEVER create S3 buckets without Block Public Access enabled at account level
CloudTrail MUST be enabled in all regions with log file validation and KMS encryption
Security Group rules MUST follow least-privilege -- no 0.0.0.0/0 ingress except on port 443 for public-facing load balancers

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need Azure or GCP security guidance, not AWS
Need application-level security (XSS, SQLi, CSRF) not cloud infrastructure
Need compliance-specific controls (HIPAA, PCI-DSS, SOC 2) not general AWS security

inputs_needed.question: Which AWS service do you need to secure?
What Infrastructure-as-Code tool are you using?
